Tosafot's argument that the line lacks Rashi commentary and proper support

macro_segments.title: Prohibition on Selling Land and Attached Property to Non-Jews

Gemara's source in the verse Lo Techonem; multiple interpretations of the phrase
Tosafot's treatment of scope: biblical prohibition versus later extension to all non-Jews

micro_segments.title: Gemara's multiple derivations from Lo Techonem: dwelling, favor, and free gifts

Tosafot's qualifications: acquaintance, accompanying on a journey, and darchei shalom justify giving
The meaning of matnat chinam as undeserved gift; no strings attached versus purposeful context
Discussion of self-censorship in manuscripts: replacing goy/nochri with ovdei kochavim and knani

macro_segments.title: The Prohibition Against Giving Favor and Compliments to Non-Jews (continued)

Rav's explanation: compliments are permitted when directed to God, not to the non-Jew

macro_segments.title: The Prohibition of Looking at Beautiful Women and Sexual Thoughts

micro_segments.title: Source in Deuteronomy about protection from every evil thing; debate on whether it applies to thoughts or seminal emission

Digression on the Angel of Death: appearance, method of causing death, and mercy through non-external cutting
Continuation of looking prohibitions: colored garments, animals copulating, and professional context exceptions

macro_segments.title: The Ten-Stage Path to Divine Spirit and Resurrection of the Dead
